messages.subject: [R-pkg-devel] [External] R_MissingArg is now non-API
messages.body_plain:
Thanks -- ported now

luke

On Wed, 1 Apr 2026, Sadao Milberg wrote:

> [You don't often get email from sadao at milberg.dev. Learn why this is important at https://aka.ms/LearnAboutSenderIdentification ]
>
> Hi, I'm still getting a note on Winbuilder:
>
>     ...
>     * using R version 4.6.0 alpha (2026-03-31 r89747 ucrt)
>     ...
>     Found non-API call to R: 'R_MissingArg'
>
> I _think_ it is because the below mentioned change did not get ported over:
>
>     trunk$ svn info | grep Revision
>     Revision: 89769
>     trunk$ svn log -r89739
>     ------------------------------------------------------------------------
>     r89739 | luke | 2026-03-29 19:07:17 -0400 (Sun, 29 Mar 2026) | 1 line
>
>     Mark R_MissingArg as @apivar.
>     ------------------------------------------------------------------------
>
>     trunk$ svn cat https://svn.r-project.org/R/branches/R-4-6-branch/src/library/tools/R/sotools.R | grep -n -C 2
> "\bR_MissingArg\b"
>     679-            "R_NamespaceRegistry", "R_Srcref", "R_UnboundValue",
>     680-            "R_InBCInterpreter", "R_CurrentExpression",
>     681:            "R_CStackDir", "R_MB_CUR_MAX", "R_MissingArg",
>     682-            "R_compact_intseq_class", "R_compact_realseq_class",
>     683-            "R_num_math_threads", "R_max_num_math_threads",
>
>     trunk$ svn log -l2 https://svn.r-project.org/R/branches/R-4-6-branch/src/library/tools/R/sotools.R
>     ------------------------------------------------------------------------
>     r89728 | luke | 2026-03-26 22:24:39 -0400 (Thu, 26 Mar 2026) | 2 lines
>
>     Merge r89724 and r89725 from the trunk.
>
>     ------------------------------------------------------------------------
>     r89719 | pd | 2026-03-26 19:02:00 -0400 (Thu, 26 Mar 2026) | 1 line
>
>     Create R-4-6-branch
>     ------------------------------------------------------------------------
>
> Best,
>
> Sadao
>
>
> On Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 07:09:24 PM EDT, Luke Tierney via R-package-devel <r-package-devel at r-project.org> wrote:
>
>
> On Sun, 29 Mar 2026, Iris Simmons wrote:
>
> > I understand that R has put Rf_findVarInFrame and R_UnboundValue on the
> > non-API list in order to deny direct access to PROMSXP objects.
> >
> > What I can't understand is why R put R_MissingArg on the non-API list. I've
> > been using it to allocate functions in C, and now I'm not sure how I'm
> > going to get around it. Right now I would write:
> >
> > Does anyone know how I might get around this?
> >
> > [[alternative HTML version deleted]]
>
> You are right: R_MissingArg is needed for this so it should be in the
> variables API. It is now and should no longer generate a check NOTE.
>
> Thanks,
>
> luke
>
>
>

-- 
Luke Tierney
Ralph E. Wareham Professor of Mathematical Sciences
University of Iowa                  Phone:             319-335-3386
Department of Statistics and        Fax:               319-335-3017
    Actuarial Science
241 Schaeffer Hall                  email:   luke-tierney at uiowa.edu
Iowa City, IA 52242                 WWW:  http://www.stat.uiowa.edu/
messages.body_snippet:
Thanks -- ported now

luke

messages.subject: [R-pkg-devel] CRAN submission down?: Not getting the expected confirmation email - last submission was 8 hours ago
messages.subject_clean: [R-pkg-devel] CRAN submission down?: Not getting the expected confirmation email - last submission was 8 hours ago
messages.body_plain:
(bcc:ing the CRAN team)

Is the CRAN submission down? I submitted a package to CRAN ~30 minutes
ago via <https://cran.r-project.org/submit.html>, and I still haven't
got the expected email confirmation, which normally comes instantly.
Looking at the CRAN incoming server (e.g. https://nx10.dev/cransubs/)
it looks like the most recent submission was eight hours ago (Apr 01
14:16 server's local time) - I would expect quite a few submissions
since then.

/Henrik

PS. win-builder's processing and email notifications work just fine

messages.subject: [R-pkg-devel] CRAN submission down?: Not getting the expected confirmation email - last submission was 8 hours ago
messages.subject_clean: [R-pkg-devel] CRAN submission down?: Not getting the expected confirmation email - last submission was 8 hours ago
messages.body_plain:
Yes, the submissions are currently down. The server decided to observe Easter early ;) - the team is aware and working on it, but it may take some time depending where the fault is.

Cheers,
Simon
